sections.paragraph:
καταδιαιρέω aor. impv. 2 sg. καταδιέλε Ps 54:10; mid. aor. 3 pl. καταδιείλαντο Jo 4:2 (s. διαιρέω; Polyb. 2, 45, 1; Hero Alex. III p. 66, 2; Dionys. Hal. 4, 19; Sext. Emp., Math. 5, 23; Herm. Wr. 3, 1b; CPR I, 22, 25; 27, 21; Ps 54:10; 135:13) divide, make a distinction between τὰς τῶν καιρῶν ἀλλαγάς Dg 4:5.—DELG s.v. αἱρέω.

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. sg. = singular
  2. pl. = plural

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

CPR CPR = Corpus Papyrorum Raineri—List 4

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 516
